design_philosophy: A minimalist, distraction-free environment that evokes the intimate, tactile feeling of a physical journal. Emphasizes clean typography, generous negative space, and a calming palette to encourage deep reflection and comfortable long-form reading.

headings.description: Elegant, traditional serif for journal titles, landing page headlines, and quotes. Gives a literary, timeless feel.

body.description: Clean, highly readable geometric sans-serif for UI elements, metadata, and long-form journal content.

scale.h1: text-5xl sm:text-6xl tracking-tight leading-none font-semibold font-serif
scale.h2: text-3xl sm:text-4xl tracking-tight leading-tight font-medium font-serif

grid_strategy.landing_page: Asymmetric bento grid mixing tall and wide blocks. Use negative space aggressively.
grid_strategy.community_feed: Single-column or minimalist masonry list. Cards should have ample internal padding (p-6 or p-8) to let text breathe.
grid_strategy.dashboard: Clean, dense table or list view for managing personal entries, keeping focus on titles, privacy status, and dates.

components.navbar: Glassmorphism: backdrop-blur-xl bg-background/80 with a delicate 1px bottom border (border-border). Must include a hamburger icon on mobile.
components.buttons: Solid flat backgrounds with slight hover lift (-translate-y-px) and subtle shadow. Primary buttons use primary_accent.
components.cards: Neumorphic/Tactile variant: Flat background matching parent exactly or slightly offset, 1px subtle border, very soft shadow on hover.
components.inputs: Minimalist. For the journal editor, rely on the WYSIWYG cleanly embedded without harsh borders.
components.online_presence_dot: An 8px by 8px rounded-full dot absolute positioned bottom-right of the user avatar. border-2 border-background to separate from the avatar image. Blue (#3B82F6) for online, Dark (#333333 in light, #1A1A1A in dark) for offline.
components.wysiwyg_editor: React Quill. Must be customized to match the typography rules. Remove overly complex toolbar options to keep the writing experience clean and distraction-free.

images.description: Minimalist white notebook on brown table. Use as landing page hero image. Overlay should be bg-black/30 maximum if text is placed on top, but prefer placing it side-by-side or in an asymmetric grid block.
White notebook in white sheets. Perfect for the 'About' section or auth split-screen background.
Gray minimalist journal books. Use in the feature section highlighting the digital journaling experience.
Woman writing by a sunlit window. Use as a banner for the 'Community Feed' or 'Public Journals' section to humanize the social experience.

motion.page_load: Fade in with slight upward translation (translate-y-4 to translate-y-0) staggered by 100ms for lists/feed items.
motion.interaction: Micro-interactions on buttons, like/comment actions (scale up 1.05x on active/click).

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text meets APCA/WCAG standards against background. Check primary_accent and text_primary against surface backgrounds.
accessibility.testing_attributes: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute formatted as kebab-case and role-based (e.g., data-testid='login-button', data-testid='journal-feed-item').

instructions_to_main_agent: Import 'Cormorant Garamond' and 'Manrope' fonts from Google Fonts in the global CSS.
Configure Tailwind CSS to use the provided hex codes from the 'colors' section. Ensure CSS variables handle the light/dark mode switch gracefully on the :root and .dark classes.

Use Shadcn UI for complex components (Dialogs, Dropdowns, Tabs for Auth) but override their default Vercel-like styling to match the earthy, minimalist aesthetic (softer borders, specific typography).
For the individual Journal Post reading view, strictly enforce max-w-2xl to maintain an optimal reading line length. Add ample vertical spacing between paragraphs.
Style the React Quill editor to blend seamlessly with the app. The editor area should feel like an infinite canvas or a clean sheet of paper, not a boxed-in form field.
Ensure the responsive navbar features a hamburger menu on mobile that opens a clean, full-screen or slide-out overlay.
When rendering user profiles and comments, strictly follow the presence indicator rule: blue dot for online, dark dot for offline.
Do not use placeholder images. If you need user avatars, use a generated generic avatar or UI fallback initials.
Always apply 'data-testid' to actionable elements and important assertions to ensure testability.
